sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root מָרַר).-

(1) subst. a drop, so called from flowing down (see מָרַר No. 1 ), Isa 40:15.

(2) adj. f. מָרָה bitter, Isa 5:20 Pro 27:7 bitter, acrid (fcharf), of brackish water, Exo 15:23. Neutr. as a substantive, bitterness (of death), 1Sa 15:32. Metaph.-(a) sad, sorrowful, Eze 3:14 often used of the mind, Job 21:25. מַר נֶפֶשׁ adj. sad of soul, 1Sa 1:10 22:2 and subst. sadness, Job 7:11 10:1 .-(b) bitter, of a cry or weeping, Germ. bitterlich. צְעָקָה גְדֹולָה וּמָרָה “a loud and bitter cry,” Gen 27:34 Est 4:1 Eze 27:31, מִסְפֵּד מַר “a bitter (violent) lamentation;” also used of a bitter fate, Pro 5:4 Amo 8:10. Adv. מַר Isa 33:7, and מָרָה Eze 27:30, bitterly.-(c) fierce, i.e. vehement, powerful, raging, i.q. Arab. مرير (whence it is at the same time manifest how עַז strong, Jud. 14:14 , can be opposed to sweet), Hab 1:6 מַר נֶפֶשׁ id. Jud. 18:25 2Sa 17:8.-(d) destructive, pernicious, Psa 64:4 Jer 2:19. מֵי הַפָּרִים bitter waters, i.e. which would be destructive to the perjured wife, Num 5:18, 19 .
